hero.headline: NIU Online MBA in International Business: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Noida International University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with an International Business specialisation. NAAC A+, WES recognised. Total all-in fee Rs 97,600 after 25% Early Bird discount. International Marketing, International Business Laws, Export Import Documentation, Trading Blocks, Inter-Cultural Management, and International Financial Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: NIU Online MBA International Business: NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, WES recognised, Rs 97,600 all-in after 25% Early Bird. International Business depth with International Marketing, Business Laws, Logistics, Export Import Documentation, Inter-Cultural Management, and International Financial Management. Budget-friendly for professionals in export, trade, and global business roles.

about.body:
The NIU Online MBA in International Business is offered by Noida International University, Greater Noida. NIU holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The degree is WES recognised, which is particularly relevant for an international business specialisation.

The specialisation covers 9 dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects including International Marketing Management, International Regulatory Environment, International Business Laws, International Logistics, Export Import Documentation, Trading Blocks and Foreign Trade Framework, Inter-Cultural Management, International Financial Management, and International Financial System. This curriculum targets professionals in export-import, global trade, international supply chain, and cross-border business roles.

NIU is not NIRF ranked and does not carry the brand weight of established international business programmes at Amity or Symbiosis. The programme's advantage is the Rs 97,600 all-in fee with WES recognition. For professionals in Delhi-NCR's export hubs and trade zones who need a credentialed MBA for international trade careers, NIU offers solid subject coverage at a fraction of competing programmes.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic frameworks, global competitive analysis, and international market entry strategies.

sem3Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem3Subjects.description: Study business law, international trade regulations, and legal frameworks for cross-border transactions.

sem3Subjects.name: International Marketing Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design international marketing strategies, global brand positioning, cross-border pricing, and export marketing plans.

sem3Subjects.name: International Regulatory Environment
sem3Subjects.description: Study WTO frameworks, bilateral trade agreements, tariff structures, and international trade compliance requirements.

sem3Subjects.name: International Business Laws
sem3Subjects.description: Apply international commercial law, INCOTERMS, cross-border dispute resolution, and international contract management.

sem3Subjects.name: International Logistics
sem3Subjects.description: Manage global supply chains, freight forwarding, customs clearance, and international shipping documentation.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
sem4Subjects.description: Study international venture planning, export startup models, and cross-border business development.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing and E-Commerce
sem4Subjects.description: Apply cross-border e-commerce strategies, international digital marketing, and global marketplace operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Export Import Documentation
sem4Subjects.description: Prepare export-import documentation including bills of lading, letters of credit, customs declarations, and trade certificates.

sem4Subjects.name: Trading Blocks and Foreign Trade Framework
sem4Subjects.description: Study ASEAN, EU, NAFTA/USMCA trading blocs, India's FTAs, and foreign trade policy frameworks.

sem4Subjects.name: Inter-Cultural Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage cross-cultural teams, international business etiquette, cultural intelligence, and global workforce diversity.

sem4Subjects.name: International Financial Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply foreign exchange management, international capital budgeting, cross-border treasury, and multinational financial planning.

sem4Subjects.name: International Financial System
sem4Subjects.description: Study global financial institutions, IMF, World Bank, international capital markets, and cross-border payment systems.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all NIU Online MBA specialisations. Strategic Management and Legal Aspects of Business are common Sem 3 subjects. Entrepreneurship Development and Digital Marketing and E-Commerce are common Sem 4 subjects.

whoHires.body:
International Business MBA graduates target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Export-import firms, International trading companies, Logistics and freight forwarding, FMCG (global operations), BFSI (forex and trade finance), Consulting (international strategy)

**Entry-level roles:** Export Executive, International Business Development Associate, Trade Documentation Officer, Forex Analyst, Import Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Export Manager, International Business Manager, Trade Finance Manager, Global Supply Chain Manager, International Marketing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP International Operations, Head of Global Trade, Director International Business, Chief Commercial Officer (Export)

**Typical hiring organisations:** Tata International, Adani Ports, Mahindra & Mahindra (International), Reliance Industries (Export), HDFC Bank (Trade Finance), ICICI Bank (Forex), DHL, FedEx, Maersk India, DGFT-linked exporters

skills.body:
The NIU International Business specialisation builds skills tested by export, trade, and global business employers:

**Trade and commerce skills:**
- Export Import Documentation: bills of lading, letters of credit, customs forms, DGFT documentation, and trade certificate preparation for export executive and trade documentation roles
- International Logistics: freight forwarding, customs clearance, global supply chain management, and shipping coordination for logistics and supply chain roles
- Trading Blocks and Foreign Trade Framework: FTA analysis, tariff impact assessment, and trade policy compliance for trade policy and international business development roles

**International finance skills:**
- International Financial Management: forex management, international capital budgeting, and cross-border treasury for trade finance and treasury roles
- International Financial System: global capital markets, IMF/World Bank operations, and international payment systems for financial services roles

**Strategy and culture skills:**
- International Marketing Management: global brand positioning, export marketing, and cross-border pricing for international marketing roles
- Inter-Cultural Management: cultural intelligence, cross-cultural negotiation, and global team management for multinational operations roles

**Soft skills:** Cross-border negotiation, international stakeholder communication, trade compliance reporting, and multicultural team leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NIU Online MBA International Business against peers:

**vs Galgotias Online MBA (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is slightly cheaper and NCR-based. NIU International Business offers 9 dedicated international trade subjects including Export Import Documentation and INCOTERMS. Both lack NIRF ranking. For trade-specific careers, NIU has stronger subject depth.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA (Rs 94,000-98,000):** Near-identical fee bracket. Both lack NIRF ranking. Compare specific international business subject availability. NIU's trade documentation and international logistics coverage is a differentiator.

**vs IGNOU MBA (Rs 66,000):** IGNOU is cheaper with government brand but has limited international business specialisation depth. NIU offers 9 dedicated subjects covering trade, logistics, and international finance.

**vs Amity Online MBA International Business (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is NIRF #22 with strong international business alumni network at 2.3x the cost. For MNC hiring where brand matters, Amity justifies the premium. NIU wins on affordability.

**vs CU Online MBA (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NIRF #19 with PwC partnerships. CU costs Rs 67,400 more. For recruiter recognition at large MNCs, CU is the stronger choice.

**vs LPU Online MBA International Business (Rs 1,61,000):** LPU is NIRF #31 with zero-cost EMI. NIU is Rs 63,400 cheaper. NIU wins on affordability; LPU offers better brand and EMI structure.

fees.body:
The NIU Online MBA in International Business fee structure:

**After 25% Early Bird Discount (all-in):**
- Tuition: Rs 88,500
- Examination fee: Rs 8,000 (Rs 2,000 per semester x 4)
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time, serves as application fee)
- Total all-in cost: Rs 97,600

**Standard fee (before scholarship):**
- Semester fee: Rs 29,500 per semester
- Examination fee: Rs 2,000 per semester
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time)

**EMI option:**
EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate and EMI terms with the admissions team before committing.

**Scholarships:** No scholarships beyond the 25% Early Bird discount. No bundled perks or certification add-ons.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with NIU each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Noida International University (NIU) and carries the MBA designation. The International Business specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is WES recognised for international credential evaluation, which is particularly valuable for international business professionals planning overseas career moves.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from NIU Online MBA International Business students. Ratings from post-completion feedback.

items.name: Harsh
items.liked: Export Import Documentation and International Logistics subjects were directly useful for my export firm role. The Rs 97,600 fee made this affordable.
items.disliked: NIU brand is not recognised at large MNCs compared to Amity or Symbiosis. No international exchange or study tour component.
items.body: For export-import professionals, the subject depth is solid. The 9 international business subjects cover trade documentation, logistics, and international finance. Brand recognition is limited for MNC roles, but for SME export businesses and trading firms, the degree is valid and affordable.

items.name: Ritika
items.liked: Inter-Cultural Management and International Financial Management were practical. WES recognition was a key factor for my Canada immigration plans.
items.disliked: EMI terms were not clearly zero-cost. Career services for international business roles were minimal.
items.body: The WES recognition adds genuine value for international business professionals considering overseas moves. The subjects cover both trade operations and global strategy. Confirm EMI interest rates before enrolling. Self-sourced job search is necessary for international roles.

items.name: Varun
items.liked: Trading Blocks and International Business Laws subjects gave good foundations. Fee was the lowest among NAAC A+ international business MBAs I compared.
items.disliked: No industry tie-ups with DGFT or export councils. Practical trade simulation exercises were limited.
items.body: At Rs 97,600, this covers more international business subjects than most budget MBAs. The legal and regulatory subjects are well-structured. For professionals already in trade roles, this adds a formal credential. For fresh graduates targeting MNC international roles, stronger brands may be needed.

faqs.question: Does NIU Online MBA International Business cover export-import documentation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Export Import Documentation is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering bills of lading, letters of credit, customs declarations, and DGFT trade certificates. This is directly applicable to export executive and trade documentation roles.

faqs.question: Is NIU Online MBA WES recognised for international careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. The NIU Online MBA is WES recognised, allowing credential evaluation for international employment and immigration. This is particularly relevant for the International Business specialisation.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for NIU Online MBA International Business?
faqs.answer: Rs 97,600 all-in after the 25% Early Bird discount. This includes Rs 88,500 tuition, Rs 8,000 exam fees, and Rs 1,100 registration fee. No additional charges.

faqs.question: Does NIU International Business MBA cover INCOTERMS and trade law?
faqs.answer: Yes. International Business Laws covers INCOTERMS, international commercial law, cross-border dispute resolution, and international contract management. International Regulatory Environment covers WTO frameworks and trade agreements.

faqs.question: Is NIU Online MBA International Business suitable for export professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme covers export-import documentation, international logistics, trade regulations, and international financial management. These subjects are directly applicable to export executive, trade manager, and international business development roles.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A+ international business MBA with 9 dedicated trade and global business subjects at Rs 97,600 all-in.
You are an export-import or trade professional in Delhi-NCR who prioritises affordability and WES recognition.
You want trade documentation, international logistics, and international finance coverage at a sub-1-lakh price.
consider: If MNC brand recognition matters for international business roles, Amity (Rs 2.25L, NIRF #22) or CU (Rs 1.65L, NIRF #19) are stronger choices.
If you want the cheapest government-backed MBA, IGNOU at Rs 66,000 is available, though without dedicated international business subjects.
If zero-cost EMI is essential, LPU offers guaranteed zero-cost 24-month EMI. NIU's EMI terms may carry interest.
